channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Apple’s new parental controls are for keeping Apple out of trouble
item.description: When Apple put child safety front and center at WWDC on Monday, its stated goal was helping parents fine-tune their kids' online experiences and avoid excessive screen time. But amid a global debate over internet regulation, its latest updates also looked like a defensive move in a brewing fight against Meta and other app developers. […]

item.title: ‘Regulation by litigation is often overlooked as a regulatory tool’: Just how much responsibility should AI companies have on their users’ wellbeing?
item.description: AI chatbots have been likened to social media, leading to addiction and potentially worse. I explore how they could be regulated.

item.title: OpenAI files for IPO, following Anthropic
item.description: OpenAI on Monday checked off a preliminary step in the IPO race that it and rival Anthropic have been competing in for the better part of a year: The company announced it has confidentially submitted a Form S-1 with the US Securities and Exchange Commission, following Anthropic's decision to do the same on June 1st. […]

item.title: iOS 27 : voici toutes les nouveautés pour iPhone, de Siri AI aux photos retouchées par IA
item.description: iOS 27 ne mise pas sur une fonction choc, mais sur une avalanche de nouveautés tournées autour de trois idées : un Siri reconstruit, de l'IA dans presque toutes les apps, et un système qui va enfin plus vite. On fait le tour complet.

item.title: OpenAI Confidentially Files for IPO on the Heels of SpaceX and Anthropic
item.description: The ChatGPT maker announced it has filed paperwork to go public, just a week after rival Anthropic took the same step.

item.title: Siri AI et iOS 27 : le récap complet de la WWDC 2026 d’Apple
item.description: Apple a présenté le nouveau Siri AI pendant le keynote de la WWDC 2026. Voici le récapitulatif de toutes les annonces très axées autour d'Apple Intelligence.

item.title: Following Anthropic, OpenAI files confidentially for IPO
item.description: The filing comes a little more than a week after its main rival, Anthropic, also filed to go public, ramping up the race between the two AI firms.

item.title: Apple abandonne une montre de 2023 alors que cet iPhone de 2019 survit
item.description: iOS 27 maintient une compatibilité jusqu'à des modèles sortis il y a sept ans, mais la mise à jour des Apple Watch abandonne violemment un modèle commercialisé en 2023.

item.title: Polymarket and Kalshi Say Influencer Partners Can’t Deny Election Results, Actually
item.description: Social media posts questioning the integrity of LA’s mayoral election were labeled “paid partnerships.” Then Kalshi and Polymarket told creators to delete them.

item.title: Apple plays catch-up at WWDC
item.description: Apple spent much of its WWDC keynote highlighting fixes, performance improvements, and long-requested features before unveiling its upgraded AI-powered Siri, signaling that the company wants users to see AI as just one part of a broader effort to improve its software.

item.title: Google cuts the price of its AI Plus plan and doubles the storage
item.description: The AI subscription now starts at $5 per month.

item.title: Apple is dropping support for some pretty modern Watch models
item.description: Three smartwatch models from 2022 and 2023 won't run watchOS 27.

item.title: The Agentic Reckoning: Enterprise AI organizations have a runtime problem, not a model problem — and most are building the wrong solution
item.description: In Q1 2026, VentureBeat's Pulse Research surfaced the “Governance Mirage”: the gap between the governance org charts enterprises had drawn and the control layers they had actually built. Forty-three percent said a central team owned AI governance; 23% couldn't agree on who owned it at all; and 31% named vendor opacity as the single biggest obstacle.This new wave of research asks the next question: Once you've admitted the governance problem, what breaks first when you try to fix it? The answer from our respondents is unambiguous. The failure point is not the model. It's the runtime.Enterprises...

item.title: The Dyson Supersonic Travel is proof you don't have to sacrifice hair health on holiday
item.description: Almost all of the Dyson power and performance for half the price in a third of the size

item.title: The best Prime Day speaker deals to shop early: Sonos, JBL, and more
item.description: Shop the best early Amazon Prime Day speaker deals from Sonos, Sony, Anker Soundcore, JBL, and more.

item.title: iOS 27 : l’iPhone change désormais vos mots de passe faibles tout seul, en naviguant sur les sites à votre place
item.description: Dans iOS 27, l'app Mots de passe ne se contente plus de repérer vos codes faibles : elle les change toute seule, en naviguant sur les sites à votre place. C'est l'une des fonctions les plus marquantes de la keynote, et l'une des plus risquées : confier à une IA le soin de modifier seul les accès à vos comptes.

item.title: Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
item.description: As AI experimentation grows more expensive, Apple is waiving cloud API costs for developers with fewer than 2 million first-time App Store downloads.

item.title: Waymo bought Apple’s self-driving car proving ground for $220M
item.description: Waymo has acquired a massive 5,500-acre proving ground in Arizona owned by Route 14 Investment Partners LLC, a Delaware shell company associated with Apple, according to documents filed with Maricopa County.

item.title: 7 new iPhone features coming to your phone in iOS 27 — from the new Siri AI to big Liquid Glass upgrades
item.description: Apple has officially revealed iOS 27, its big iPhone software upgrade for 2026. But what new features will it bring? Here are all the highlights.

item.title: 56% of new US data centers could be built in "high risk" disaster-prone states with insurers warning of $800 billion investment exposed
item.description: Over half of planned US data centers are in high-risk, disaster-prone states, but the weather is only one consideration.

item.title: Why Repair Cafes are becoming more popular amid the anti-consumerism movement
item.description: The ‘right to repair’ movement wants consumers to be empowered to repair their own products. On a drizzly Saturday morning late last month, the basement of the New Paltz United Methodist Church filled with old lamps, blunt knives, malfunctioning sound mixers and balky zippers.

item.title: WWDC 2026: Everything revealed in 13 minutes
item.description: Catch the biggest announcements from WWDC 2026, including Siri upgrades, Apple Intelligence features, and the new Liquid Glass design.

item.title: The 10 coolest laptops we saw at Computex 2026
item.description: We rounded up the 10 most impressive laptops from Computex 2026, including gaming rigs, ultraportables, two-in-ones, and AI-powered PCs.

item.title: Siri AI’s new voice customization could have trumped Alexa+, but scant HomeOS updates stall the win for Apple
item.description: Siri AI is packed to the brim with new AI features, bringing it up to speed with much of its competition. One feature, however, will take it leagues ahead in my home.

item.title: iOS 27 : comment installer la bêta dès maintenant sur votre iPhone (mais faites attention)
item.description: Apple vient de présenter iOS 27 lors de la WWDC 2026, et la première bêta développeur est mise en ligne dès ce lundi 8 juin. Voici comment l’installer sur votre iPhone, mais faites attention.

item.title: 44 things coming to your Apple devices that you might have missed
item.description: This year's WWDC keynote was all about AI. But with all the attention on Apple Intelligence and Siri AI, the company breezed by - or neglected to mention - a bunch of cool, smaller features across its new updates. I've rounded up a bunch of them right here. The new operating systems are available in […]

item.title: Apple permet de changer l’angle de vue d’une photo… après l’avoir prise
item.description: Changer le point de vue de sa photo a posteriori, c'est la nouveauté présentée par Apple lors de la WWDC 2026.

item.title: iOS 27 can run on phones as old as the iPhone 11
item.description: Apple's focus on efficiency and performance improvements is good news for older iPhones.

item.title: A trip to the center of Knicks merch mania
item.description: We visited the epicenter of NBA bootleg gear, where the sellers of AI-generated t-shirts and airbrushed tanks are scrambling to keep up with fandom. As the 90 degree sun blared in Midtown Manhattan last Friday, a crowd of dozens flooded the entrance of Madison Square Garden snaking out of the venue’s door—not for a concert or a game, but for merch. It was just five hours before game 2 of the NBA finals, and the line to snag an official jersey or a cap at the Knicks’ home arena seemed to be never-ending, despite moving quickly. ...

item.title: Every MacBook getting macOS 27: Is yours on the list?
item.description: macOS 27 Golden Gate will bring updates to Liquid Glass, Apple Intelligence, and the new Siri AI to these MacBooks and Mac devices.

item.title: Forget AI — credit rating giant feared by all countries just issued an alarming warning as Google and CloudFlare make crucial moves: "PQC spending will compete directly with AI investment"
item.description: Google and Cloudflare just accelerated their PQC targets – this is why you should too, even though AI promises more immediate returns.

item.title: Microsoft’s open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
item.description: Microsoft shut down dozens of GitHub code repositories for Azure and AI coding tools after a reported hack.

item.title: Deux vols habités français… mais où est la stratégie spatiale derrière cette annonce ?
item.description: Deux astronautes français dans l'espace : l'un à bord de l'ISS, l'autre sur la future station privée américaine Haven-1. L'annonce, faite par Emmanuel Macron lors de « Choose France », a tout d'un coup d'éclat. Mais acheter des sièges dans l'espace ne fait pas une politique spatiale. Derrière le...

item.title: Everything announced at WWDC 2026, the start of the Siri AI era
item.description: Siri AI, iOS 27, macOS 27 Golden Gate, and new safety tools were announced at the Apple WWDC 2026 keynote event.

item.title: Everything Apple Announced at WWDC 2026
item.description: Updates include a new souped-up Siri, lots of iOS enhancements, and some inkling on how an AI partnership with Google has come to power Apple’s products.

item.title: Penn Station is about to be a lot more pleasant
item.description: The architects at PAU and HOK share more details on the roomier, brighter train station. The architectural firm behind plans to remake New York City’s Penn Station unveiled its design on Monday, and renderings show a station that’s meant to be bigger, brighter, and more welcoming to the public.

item.title: How to watch Stanley Pup 2026: The NHL’s adorable puppy showdown returns tonight
item.description: Rescue dogs representing all 32 NHL teams will compete for hockey’s cutest title in the annual Stanley Pup special. Tonight, the National Hockey League’s cutest competition is back, bringing together adoptable shelter pups to celebrate the Stanley Cup and find their forever homes.

item.title: How housing market inventory is shifting across every state
item.description: PulteGroup VP Jim Zeumer: ‘We have work to do in Oregon and Washington. We have work to do to clear spec in some of our Western markets.’ Want more housing market stories from Lance Lambert’s ResiClub in your inbox? Subscribe to the ResiClub newsletter.

item.title: Apples new Notify Me feature could be the next great shopping tool
item.description: At Apple WWDC, the company announced new AI features for Safari. Notify Me, a customizable notification center, is basically a built-in price tracker. Here's what we know about the new feature dropping this fall.
